calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-20-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for and with (executor) Robert Suttice.

The Court stated that the main concern is 7.705(b) and asked if they wish to submit additional receipts. Ms. Smith stated that they cannot get additional information, that Mr. Suttice went to the bank and that it would take two weeks to receive the form needed and then an amount of time after submission of the form, that the only difference in the fees is $11.00 and asked that the be allowed to base it on the gain of sale. The Court stated that if the choice is between delay and submitting the additional information the matter can be taken under submission with time to submit. Ms. Smith stated that they have consent to the gain at the higher fee, and that they can file a declaration. The Court stated that it will give them until 5/6/16 to file the declaration and that the accounting petition will be under submission as of that date. Mr. Suttice stated that the bank said it will take a lot of time, that this has been going on for 3 years already and that he has been paying everything himself.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-11-2016 Continued To May-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-28-2016 Continued To Apr-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Greer Smith for and with (petitioner) Robert Suttice.

Ms. Greer stated that they understand that presumption applies where will was last seen with the testator, that their declaration tried to show that the will was lodged with the attorney and that he can no longer find it. Mr. Suttice reported that there Ms. Suttice had 4 children, that she would have wanted everything to be split equally however did not do this as 1 of her children has child support issues and she did not want the money to be taken away for that and a second son is deceased and had owed her money and stated that they spoke of these issues many times. Ms. Greer informed the court that the conservatorship case number is PCN-10-293488. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-13-2015 Continued To Apr-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
